subject: Chemistry
title: Nuclear Transmutation: Alchemy Achieved
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Nuclear Transmutation: Alchemy Achieved.

hook.text: In 1919, Rutherford turned nitrogen into oxygen by bombarding it with alpha particles. Transmutation was real.

buildup.text: He fired helium nuclei at nitrogen gas. Some nitrogen atoms absorbed a proton and became oxygen.

discovery.text: This was the first artificial nuclear transmutation—changing one element into another, just as alchemists dreamed.

twist.text: In 1980, scientists transmuted bismuth into gold using a particle accelerator. But it cost far more than the gold was worth.

climax.text: Alchemy's dream came true through nuclear physics—not chemical reactions. The alchemists had the right dream, wrong method.

punchline.text: Smash atoms, change elements. The alchemists' dream needed nuclear physics.

quiz.question: Who first achieved artificial transmutation of one element into another?
options: Ernest Rutherford, turning nitrogen into oxygen in 1919
Marie Curie, while studying radium in 1898
Albert Einstein, through his mass-energy equation
